title: Chicken Tandoori

ingredients.title: For the Chicken
items: 1 whole chicken (about 1.2–1.5 kg), cut into pieces

ingredients.title: For the Marinade
items: 1 cup thick yogurt (Greek yogurt works best)
2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for vibrant color, mild heat)
1–2 tsp chaat masala (optional, for authentic tang)
2 tbsp vegetable oil or mustard oil
1 tsp smoked paprika (optional, for smoky aroma)

instruction.title: 1. Prep the Chicken
steps: Wash and pat dry the chicken pieces.
Make 2–3 deep slashes in each piece to allow the marinade to penetrate.
Rub with lemon juice and salt. Let sit for 15–20 minutes.

instruction.title: 2. Make the Marinade
steps: In a large bowl, mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili, turmeric, coriander, cumin, garam masala, chaat masala, oil, and smoked paprika.
Taste and adjust salt/spices if needed.

instruction.title: 3. Marinate the Chicken
steps: Coat each chicken piece thoroughly with the marinade.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, ideally overnight for maximum flavor.

instruction.title: 4. Cooking Options
steps: Oven Method (Preferred for Home Chefs): Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F). Line a baking tray with foil and place a wire rack on top. Arrange chicken pieces on the rack. Bake for 20 minutes, then flip and bake another 15–20 minutes until chicken is cooked through. For a smoky flavor, broil for 2–3 minutes at the end.

Grill Method (Authentic Tandoor Flavor): Preheat the grill or barbecue. Grill chicken over medium-high heat for 10–12 minutes per side until cooked and slightly charred. Baste occasionally with leftover marinade.

instruction.title: 5. Finishing Touch
steps: Sprinkle chaat masala or garam masala if desired.
Garnish with coriander leaves, lemon wedges, and onion rings.

chefTips: Yogurt: Thick, unsweetened yogurt ensures a creamy, flavorful marinade.
Marination: Don’t rush it—overnight marination tenderizes and intensifies flavor.
Char: A quick broil or grill step gives authentic tandoori char marks.
Oil: Mustard oil gives a slightly pungent, traditional flavor; neutral oils work fine too.
Kashmiri chili powder: Adds vibrant color without overpowering heat.
